5 min read
Just now

--

Moonshot AI just dropped the world’s first open 3T-class model. 2.8 trillion parameters. 1 million token context. #1 on the Frontend Code Arena. Here’s everything you need to know.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

The Announcement That Shook the AI World
July 16, 2026. Moonshot AI — the Beijing-based startup backed by Alibaba — unveiled Kimi K3. A 2.8 trillion parameter model. The world’s first open-source AI in the 3-trillion-parameter class. And it just beat Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 on the Frontend Code Arena.
This isn’t an incremental update. This is a category shift. The largest open-weight model ever shipped. A direct challenge to the closed-source dominance of OpenAI and Anthropic. And it’s priced like a mid-tier model while performing at frontier level.
Here’s what K3 is, why it matters, and how to use it today.
What Kimi K3 Actually Is
K3 is a sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model with 2.8 trillion total parameters. Only 16 of 896 experts activate per token — roughly 1.8% of the pool. This keeps inference efficient despite the massive scale.
The architecture:

• Stable LatentMoE — routes each token to 16 of 896 experts
